result:
This is an impressive 85K-line terminal-based simulation platform that started as Conway's Game of Life and has grown into 130+ modes spanning cellular automata, physics, ecology, chemistry, AI, and more.

**Proposed goal: Add a Procedural Music / Generative Soundscape mode that sonifies any running simulation in real-time — mapping cell density to harmony, spatial patterns to melody, and entropy to rhythm — turning every existing mode into a listenable composition.**

The project already has a `sound.py` module and a `sonification.py` mode, but every mode is essentially a visual-only experience. A cross-cutting "sonification layer" that can attach to *any* simulation (not just its own standalone mode) would be a unique multiplier — it makes all 130+ existing modes more compelling without needing to modify each one individually. It would listen to the simulation's grid state each frame and procedurally generate terminal-bell or PCM audio output based on spatial statistics (population, cluster count, symmetry, rate of change), effectively turning the entire project into an audiovisual instrument.
